revision.text:
[[ja:船山神社]] {{Expand Japanese|船山神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Funayama Shrine | native_name = 船山神社 | image = Ob4rjjbj.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Funayama Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.628445|135.712015}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Funayama Shrine'''|船山神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|ltq=Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} ** Series ordinal: 42 ** P155: {{ill|Igami Shrine|lt=Igami Shrine|ja|猪上神社|qid=Q119929666|1=Igami Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q119929667|ltq=Q119929667|qid=Q119929667|1=Mikushi Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1346790|ltq=Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} * {{ill|Yamato Province|lt=Yamato Province|be|Ямата (правінцыя)|ca|Província de Yamato|ckb|پارێزگای یەماتۆ|cs|Provincie Jamato|de|Provinz Yamato|el|Επαρχία Γιαμάτο|en|Yamato Province|es|Provincia de Yamato|fa|ولایت یاماتو|fi|Yamaton provinssi|fr|Province de Yamato|gl|Provincia de Yamato|hak|Yamato-koet|id|Provinsi Yamato|it|Yamato (provincia)|ja|大和国|ko|야마토국|lt|Jamato žemė|nl|Yamato (provincie)|no|Yamato (provins)|pt|Província de Yamato|ro|Provincia Yamato|ru|Ямато (провинция)|simple|Yamato Province|sk|Jamato (provincia)|sr|Провинција Јамато|th|แคว้นยามาโตะ|tr|Yamato (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ямато|ur|یاماتو صوبہ|zh_min_nan|Yamato-kok|zh_yue|大和國|zh|大和國|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Q11483501|ltq=Q11483501|qid=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.628445|135.712015}} == image (P18) == * Ob4rjjbj.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 180701 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917284|ltq=Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県生駒郡平群町三里字船山475番', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:船山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q119929665}} == Japanese content == {{?? | ?? = Funayama Shrine | ?? = [[????:Ob4rjjbj.jpg|250px|]]
Torii
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | ??? = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|qid=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} Misato Aza Funayama 475 | ISO = JP-29 | ??? = 34 | ??? = 37 | ??? = 42.4 | ??? = 135 | ??? = 42 | ??? = 43.3 | ?? = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}?former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | ?? = Funayama deity | ?? = Japan Nara }} '''Funayama Shrine''' (????????) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmy?ch?|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} enshrined in Misato, {{ill|lt=Heguri|Q1346790|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Ikoma District|Q971003|qid=Q971003|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1273537/1/44 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-02-05}}. Its former rank under the modern shrine ranking system was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Overview === The founding and history are unknown, but it is an old shrine listed in the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}''. ''{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmeich?|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' says the enshrined deity is unknown{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/971155/1/64 |title=Tokusen Jinmeich? |access-date=2024-4-8}}, while the shrine registry lists it as the Funayama deity{{Cite web |url=https://meta01.library.pref.nara.jp/opac/repository/repo/69/ |title=Meiji 12 Shrine Register, p.99 |access-date=2024-4-8}}. The former shrine stood about 200 meters southwestThe shrine signboard says "southeast 200m," but "southwest 200m" is correct. of the current location (east of T?k?-ji, now uninhabited, at site no.42 of the Yamato Northern 88 Pilgrimage). Around Taish? 4 it was moved to Kasuga Shrine in former Anmy?ji Village{{Cite kotobank |word=?? |accessdate=2022-09-26}}, and that shrine came to be worshipped as Funayama Shrine. To the right of the main hall is Kasuga-sha (deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}), and to the left is ?miwa-sha (deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}{{Cite book|??|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=Heguri Town History|publisher=Heguri Town Office|date=1976-04|ncid=BN04492478|pages=647-648}}; another view says Sumiyoshi Daimy?jin). It is an old shrine preserving {{ill|lt=iwakura|Q11587880|qid=Q11587880|1=Iwakura rock}} worship. A prominent yang-stone beside the worship hall was moved from the former site. Some say there was also a yin-stone there{{Cite kotobank |word=??? |access-date=2022-09-26}}{{Cite web |url=https://p52de42cb7.hatenablog.com/entry/2022/09/28/144909 |title=Former site of Funayama Shrine |access-date=2024-4-20}}. There is also a theory that this stone was intentionally set to face the yin-stone of {{ill|lt=Heguri Ishidoko Shrine|Q119929663|qid=Q119929663|1=Hegurinoiwatoko Shrine}} across the Heguri Valley. Near the 8th station area of the Yata Hills behind the shrine are three giant stones and a boat-shaped stone, worshipped as boat stones on which deities descended. These stones are also mentioned in "Jingishi-ry? vol. 8"{{Cite web|??|title=National Diet Library Digital Collections |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/815494/1/14 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-24}}, and tradition says that in Meiji 1 the Funakami Shrine once worshipped there was merged into Funayama Shrine. === Notes === {{?????}} {{Reflist|2}} === See also === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Funayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Funayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Funayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]]
